diary: Woke up to one of those perfect Seattle Sundays—coffee tasted better than usual, I think. Spent the morning writing actual letters by hand, which felt like a small rebellion against my phone. Then I headed down to Alki with my foraging basket, and the beach was gorgeous. Found some decent mushrooms tucked along the tree line, nothing fancy but enough to feel like I'd actually accomplished something. Made a simple lunch on the patio with what I'd gathered, and it tasted different somehow—maybe because I'd found it myself. Drove over to Fremont later to catch up with Sarah at Molly Moon's. We talked for nearly two hours, one of those conversations that makes you forget the world exists for a bit. Came home, lost myself in some gaming, then just sat outside watching the light fade. It was one of those rare days where nothing rushed me.
thought_process: The calm weather put me in a reflective mood from the start—no pressure, no listings demanding attention on a Sunday. I deliberately chose slower activities: the handwritten letters felt intentional, a way to connect without screens. Foraging appealed to me because it's meditative and tangible, something my real estate brain doesn't usually get to do. Meeting Sarah was a priority because I realized I'd been running on fumes lately, and I needed that grounding. The drive times gave me space to think without forcing it. By evening, I was genuinely tired in a good way, not the drained exhaustion I usually carry. The day's simplicity actually fed something in me that all my condo listings and client calls can't touch.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
